questions.original_question: 路特斯科技在售产品有哪些？价格是多少？
questions.answer:
2024年，路特斯科技在售的产品涵盖多款高端车型，价格区间主要集中在中高端豪华车市场。具体产品及价格如下：

1. **纯电动超级跑车EVIJA**：价格超过220万美元，定位为顶级豪华跑车。  
2. **纯电动智能SUV ELETRE**：属于E级别，价格超过10万美元。其中，Eletre Carbon高性能版本的起售价为229,900美元。  
3. **纯电动智能GT轿车EMEYA**：同样属于E级别，价格超过10万美元。  
4. **燃油动力跑车EMIRA**：价格超过8.5万美元，是路特斯在燃油车领域的代表车型。  

此外，路特斯还提供**Colin Chapman定制服务**，为消费者提供个性化选项和限量版车型，进一步提升了品牌的高端定位。所有车型的制造商建议零售价（MSRP）均超过10万美元，体现了路特斯在豪华车市场的强劲竞争力。

rag_info.chunk_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

rag_info.chunk_content: New York and Singapore - January 9, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, announced today that its intelligent driving arm, Lotus Robotics, will showcase numerous proprietary next-generation autonomous driving software and hardware solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, USA. The technologies on display, along with Lotus Technology Inc.’s recent receipt of approval to test conditional automated driving (“Level 3” or “L3”) functions on highways in part of China, demonstrate Lotus Technology Inc.’s progress in executing the brand’s Vision80 business transformation strategy and further advancing its global research and development prowess. The ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy software being showcased at CES provide solutions for advanced driver assistance systems, remotely assisted operation, and cloud data management respectively. The technologies have been employed in Lotus Technology Inc. vehicles, contributing to their autonomous driving capabilities. The hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, for example, both feature cutting-edge autonomous driving hardware, aviation-level security and redundancy including dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips, as well as Lotus Technology Inc.’s industry-leading cognition module, which can intelligently identify and avoid risks on the road. Lotus Robotics is also displaying hardware that it is developing as part of efforts to accelerate the adoption of self-driving technology, including the ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology’s American Depositary Shares will begin trading on the Nasdaq on February 23, 2024, under the ticker symbol “LOT.” New York and Singapore - February 20, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“L CAA”) (NASDAQ: LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm, announced today that their previously proposed business combination is expected to be completed on February 22, 2024. The combined company will retain Lotus Technology Inc.'s name as “Lotus Technology Inc.” and its American Depositary Shares (ADS) will commence trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol “LOT” on February 23, 2024. The business combination was approved by L CAA shareholders at an extraordinary general meeting held on February 2, 2024. Integrating Lotus Technology’s sports car DNA with a new generation of premium lifestyle vehicles, Lotus Technology Inc. is committed to transforming the brand into an advanced, fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider before its 80th anniversary in 2028. Lotus Technology Inc. has already set this ambitious plan in motion by launching a leading product portfolio of high-performing battery electric vehicles (“BEVs”) ahead of other global luxury automakers. These BEVs include Eletre, a new breed of all-electric hyper-SUV powered by Lotus Technology’s proprietary 800-volt EPA, and Emeya, an all-electric hyper-GT among the most advanced vehicles of its kind in the world.

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. Lotus Technology expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, with new flagship locations in Paris, London, and Seoul. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

rag_info.chunk_content: New York – Oct 17, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 7,543 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of 134%. Global allocation in terms of deliveries remained well-distributed, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World regions each contributing about 20-35% of the total deliveries. In the third quarter of 2024, the Emeya (GT, Sedan) was delivered to Europe, whilst the Eletre (SUV) reached markets including the GCC, Japan, and Korea. In September, Lotus Technology Inc. revealed the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market with a starting price of $229,900. The deliveries of the Eletre Carbon are expected to commence in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter.

rag_info.chunk_content:
How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Exhibit No.   Description 
summary: Lotus Technology has revealed promising preliminary delivery results for 2024, showcasing a notable rise in vehicle deliveries driven by robust demand for its electric vehicles. This increase is linked to improved production capabilities and effective marketing strategies. The company is focused on maintaining this growth trajectory and enhancing its market presence in the near future. Additionally, they provided insights into specific models and future projections, underscoring their dedication to innovation and customer satisfaction.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content:
How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 1. How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year?
summary: In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology demonstrated exceptional growth, reporting total revenue of $398 million, which reflects a remarkable year-on-year increase of 206%. The company's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained robust, exceeding $100,000, and significantly outpacing growth in both the automotive industry and the luxury car segment. This surge in revenue was accompanied by an improvement in gross margin, which rose to 13%, up from 5% during the same period in 2023.

Lotus achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, marking a staggering 239% increase year-on-year, thereby establishing itself as the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car manufacturers. Deliveries were well-distributed across major markets, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of World each contributing between 20-30% to the total.

The company has also embarked on strategic collaborations with leading automotive brands, which are expected to enhance technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and technology development services. The growth in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware is anticipated to create additional opportunities for paid software subscription revenue.

Furthermore, the introduction of the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service has allowed Lotus to offer personalized options and designer customizations, catering to evolving customer demands. This initiative is expected to strengthen the brand's luxury positioning and positively influence long-term profit margins.

Overall, Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of 2024 reflects strong financial growth, strategic market positioning, and a commitment to enhancing customer experience, setting a solid foundation for continued success in the luxury automotive sector.

summary: In the first half of 2024, the brand experienced its best performance to date, driven by a well-balanced global strategy and significant deliveries in the US after its market re-entry. The introduction of tailored services and growth in technology-related revenue are expected to improve profit margins in the future. However, the annual delivery target has been revised due to short-term challenges from trade uncertainties and tariff hikes. Additionally, the brand has launched the "Win26" Plan, which aims for positive operating cash flow and EBITDA by 2026. Lotus Technology Inc. also reported its unaudited financial results for the second quarter and the first half of 2024, highlighting its position in the global intelligent and luxury mobility sector, although specific financial details were not disclosed.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Over 7,600 vehicles were delivered in the first nine months of 2024, a year-on-year increase of 136%. Total revenue reached $653 million, more than doubling year-on-year. Revenue from the intelligent driving business generated from customers other than Lotus surged to $11 million, with a year-on-year growth of 450%. Lotus Technology launched Hyper Hybrid EV technology that provides high performance with a combined range of over 1,100 kilometers. New York – Nov 21, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and first nine months ended September 30, 2024.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Third
Quarter and First Nine Months 2024 Financial Results 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has released its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and the first nine months of 2024, concluding on September 30, 2024. During this period, the company achieved a remarkable milestone by delivering over 7,600 vehicles, which represents a substantial year-on-year increase of 136%. Total revenue for the company reached $653 million, more than doubling compared to the same period last year. 

A significant highlight of the financial results was the performance of the intelligent driving business, which generated $11 million in revenue from customers other than Lotus, showcasing an impressive year-on-year growth of 450%. 

In addition to these financial achievements, Lotus Technology announced the launch of its Hyper Hybrid EV technology on November 21, 2024. This innovative technology promises high performance with a combined driving range exceeding 1,100 kilometers, further solidifying the company's commitment to advancing electric vehicle technology.

Overall, Lotus Technology's third-quarter and nine-month results reflect strong growth and innovation within the company, positioning it favorably in the competitive automotive market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. Lotus Technology expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, with new flagship locations in Paris, London, and Seoul. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Financial Results 
summary: In 2023, Lotus Tech achieved significant milestones, reporting a revenue of $679 million, driven by strong sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company recorded a gross profit margin of 15% in its inaugural year of BEV deliveries, benefiting from an asset-light production model. Lotus Tech expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, including new flagship locations in major cities like Paris, London, and Seoul, and delivered a record 6,970 vehicles, with BEVs making up 63% of total deliveries. The fourth quarter alone saw a remarkable 92% increase in revenue, reaching $361 million, and nearly 110% growth in vehicle deliveries compared to the previous quarter, largely due to the success of the Eletre lifestyle BEV.

Looking forward, Lotus Tech anticipates a nearly threefold increase in deliveries in 2024, targeting a total of 26,000 units while aiming to enhance its gross margin to between 17-19%. The company is set to expand its Vision80 strategy, which seeks to transform Lotus into a fully electric and luxury mobility provider by 2028. Deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, have begun in China, with European deliveries expected in the third quarter. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed optimism about the company's progress, emphasizing a commitment to long-term growth and value creation for customers and investors as they leverage their luxury brand and advanced technology.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: The financial overview of Lotus Technology Inc. highlights significant growth and challenges across various metrics for the periods ending June 30, 2024, compared to the same periods in 2023. The bar chart analysis indicates a remarkable increase in financial figures, with quarterly revenues rising from **$130 million** in 1H23 to **$398 million** in 1H24, and from **$110 million** in 2Q23 to **$225 million** in 2Q24. Deliveries also surged, with 4,873 units delivered in 1H24, marking a **239%** year-over-year increase.

Despite these positive trends in revenue and deliveries, the company faced escalating costs, leading to increased operating losses and net losses. The operating loss expanded to **$438 million** in 1H24, and the net loss reached **$460 million**, reflecting ongoing investments and operational challenges. The adjusted EBITDA also showed a decline, indicating that while revenues grew, the company is still navigating significant financial hurdles.

In terms of assets and liabilities, total assets increased from **$1.58 billion** to **$2.51 billion**, driven by growth in current assets. However, total liabilities also rose, resulting in a narrower shareholders' deficit, which improved from **$(571 million)** to **$(202 million)**.

Additionally, the company is focused on strategic growth, as evidenced by its ambitious production targets, aiming for over **30,000 units** by 2026, and a gross profit margin exceeding **20%**. The vehicle lineup includes exclusive models like the Emeya x Blossom Enchantment and the Evija x Fittipaldi, emphasizing Lotus' commitment to luxury and performance.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1:** Deliveries in 1H2024 reached 4,873 units, compared to 1,439 units in 1H2023, reflecting an impressive 239% YoY increase. In 2Q2024, deliveries totaled 2,679 units, up 128% from 1,175 units in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 2:** Revenues grew significantly to $398 million in 1H2024 from $130 million in 1H2023, representing a 206% YoY increase. Revenues in 2Q2024 amounted to $225 million, a 103% increase from $111 million in 2Q2023.

- **Row 9:** Adjusted EBITDA in 1H2024 stood at a loss of $382 million, up 15% from $333 million in 1H2023. In 2Q2024, it was a loss of $177 million, almost unchanged (a 1% decrease) from $178 million in 2Q2023.

[Table Level]
- **Table Title:** Iconic Brand – Historical Achievements of Lotus Technology Inc.
- **Table Summary:** This visual showcases the key milestones and achievements of Lotus Technology Inc. across its history, from 1957 to 2023, highlighting specific innovations, victories, and recognitions in motorsport and luxury design. It also provides a summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s accolades, including championship wins, Grand Prix victories, and prestigious awards.
- **Context:** The context emphasizes Lotus Technology Inc.’s legacy as a leader in motorsport innovation and luxury category recognition, underlining its transformation and status as a globally recognized brand.
- **Special Notes:** All years are in chronological order, and specific achievements are tied to models or events. The laurels summarize Lotus Technology Inc.'s accomplishments in motorsport (e.g., F1 Constructors’ World Championships) and Le Mans victories.

[Row Level]
- **Row 1:** In 1957, the Lotus Eleven achieved its "First Le Mans Win" in the 750cc Index Class, cementing Lotus Technology Inc.'s presence in endurance racing.
- **Row 2:** In 1960, the Lotus Type 18 secured its "First Grand Prix Win" at Monte Carlo, marking Lotus Technology Inc.'s success in Formula 1 racing.
- **Row 3:** In 1963, the Lotus Type 25 achieved a double milestone by winning the "First Formula 1 Constructors' Championship" as well as the "First Formula 1 Drivers' Championship," showcasing the excellence of the car and team.
- **Row 4:** In 1965, the Lotus Type 38 claimed "First INDY 500 Victory," becoming the first rear-engine car to win this prestigious event, revolutionizing the race landscape.
- **Row 5:** In 1978, the Lotus Sunbeam won the "First World Rally Constructors' Championship," demonstrating Lotus Technology Inc.'s versatility in both on-road and rally formats.
- **Row 6:** In 2019, Lotus Technology Inc. was recognized as the "Luxury Brand of the Year" at the prestigious Luxury Briefing Awards, celebrating its influence in the premium automotive industry.
- **Row 7:** In 2020, the Lotus Evija secured the "2020 MUSE Global Design Awards," highlighting its groundbreaking innovation in design.
- **Row 8:** In 2023, the Lotus Eletre was named the "2023 GQ SUV of the Year," reflecting its impact in the SUV market as a luxury electric vehicle.
- **Row 9:** In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. earned the "Carwow Brand Listing of Lotus," establishing its brand impact and visibility for that year.

**Achievements Summary (Laurel Section):**
- Lotus Technology Inc. has won **7 FIA Formula 1 Constructors' World Championships** and **6 FIA Formula 1 Drivers' World Championships**, demonstrating dominance in F1 racing.
- Lotus Technology Inc. boasts an impressive **81 FIA Formula 1 Grand Prix Wins**, along with **9 Le Mans Wins** in class, testament to its endurance racing legacy.
- Lotus Technology Inc. has achieved **1 Indianapolis 500 Grand Prix Win** and **1 FIA World Rally Constructors' Championship**, highlighting its innovations across multiple motorsport disciplines.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Announces Delivery Updates
of First Ten Months of 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has reported its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024, achieving a total of 8,631 vehicles delivered. The primary markets driving these deliveries were Europe and China, which accounted for approximately 35% and 25% of total deliveries, respectively. In October, the company launched an open beta test for its urban Navigate on Autopilot (NOA) feature in key cities and introduced several over-the-air (OTA) intelligent driving functions in Europe. 

Additionally, Lotus Robotics, the company's division focused on intelligent driving solutions, is providing services not only for Lotus vehicles but also for other major automotive manufacturers, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto. The company has also been recognized for its commitment to sustainability, receiving the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category, making it the only automotive industry recipient of this accolade.

It is important to note that the delivery figures are based on invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the U.S. market, and may differ slightly from the company's financial accounting records. Overall, Lotus Technology continues to make significant strides in vehicle delivery, technological advancements, and sustainability initiatives.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. is a foreign company that files a FORM 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to provide updates on significant corporate events and financial performance. This report is essential for ensuring that U.S. investors have access to timely information about the company's operations, particularly as it pertains to its financial health and corporate governance.

For the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology reported substantial growth in key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. Deliveries increased by 239%, reaching 4,873 units, while revenues surged to $398 million, a 206% rise. Despite these gains, the company faced rising costs, leading to an operating loss of $438 million and a net loss of $460 million for the first half of 2024. The adjusted net loss also increased, reflecting ongoing financial challenges.

The company's balance sheet as of June 30, 2024, showed total assets rising significantly to $2.5 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. However, total liabilities also grew, resulting in a shareholders' deficit that improved but remained negative. Notably, the company eliminated mezzanine equity, indicating a shift in its capital structure.

Lotus Technology's product lineup includes several high-performance vehicle models, with projections indicating continued growth and profitability by 2026. The company anticipates producing over 30,000 units and achieving a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. Recent product launches, including limited-edition models, highlight the brand's focus on luxury and exclusivity.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates significant revenue growth and operational advancements, it continues to navigate challenges related to rising costs and net losses. The financial data presented underscores both the company's potential and the need for careful management of its financial resources as it aims for future profitability.

title: 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. is a foreign company that files a FORM 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to provide updates on significant corporate events and financial performance. This report is essential for ensuring that U.S. investors have access to timely information about the company's operations, particularly as it pertains to its financial health and corporate governance.

For the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology reported substantial growth in key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. Deliveries increased by 239%, reaching 4,873 units, while revenues surged to $398 million, a 206% rise. Despite these gains, the company faced rising costs, leading to an operating loss of $438 million and a net loss of $460 million for the first half of 2024. The adjusted net loss also increased, reflecting ongoing financial challenges.

The company's balance sheet as of June 30, 2024, showed total assets rising significantly to $2.5 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. However, total liabilities also grew, resulting in a shareholders' deficit that improved but remained negative. Notably, the company eliminated mezzanine equity, indicating a shift in its capital structure.

Lotus Technology's product lineup includes several high-performance vehicle models, with projections indicating continued growth and profitability by 2026. The company anticipates producing over 30,000 units and achieving a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. Recent product launches, including limited-edition models, highlight the brand's focus on luxury and exclusivity.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates significant revenue growth and operational advancements, it continues to navigate challenges related to rising costs and net losses. The financial data presented underscores both the company's potential and the need for careful management of its financial resources as it aims for future profitability.

For the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology reported substantial growth in key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. Deliveries increased by 239%, reaching 4,873 units, while revenues surged to $398 million, a 206% rise. Despite these gains, the company faced rising costs, leading to an operating loss of $438 million and a net loss of $460 million for the first half of 2024. The adjusted net loss also increased, reflecting ongoing financial challenges.

The company's balance sheet as of June 30, 2024, showed total assets rising significantly to $2.5 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. However, total liabilities also grew, resulting in a shareholders' deficit that improved but remained negative. Notably, the company eliminated mezzanine equity, indicating a shift in its capital structure.

Lotus Technology's product lineup includes several high-performance vehicle models, with projections indicating continued growth and profitability by 2026. The company anticipates producing over 30,000 units and achieving a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. Recent product launches, including limited-edition models, highlight the brand's focus on luxury and exclusivity.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates significant revenue growth and operational advancements, it continues to navigate challenges related to rising costs and net losses. The financial data presented underscores both the company's potential and the need for careful management of its financial resources as it aims for future profitability.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York and Singapore - January 9, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, announced today that its intelligent driving arm, Lotus Robotics, will showcase numerous proprietary next-generation autonomous driving software and hardware solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, USA. The technologies on display, along with Lotus Technology Inc.’s recent receipt of approval to test conditional automated driving (“Level 3” or “L3”) functions on highways in part of China, demonstrate Lotus Technology Inc.’s progress in executing the brand’s Vision80 business transformation strategy and further advancing its global research and development prowess. The ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy software being showcased at CES provide solutions for advanced driver assistance systems, remotely assisted operation, and cloud data management respectively. The technologies have been employed in Lotus Technology Inc. vehicles, contributing to their autonomous driving capabilities. The hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, for example, both feature cutting-edge autonomous driving hardware, aviation-level security and redundancy including dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips, as well as Lotus Technology Inc.’s industry-leading cognition module, which can intelligently identify and avoid risks on the road. Lotus Robotics is also displaying hardware that it is developing as part of efforts to accelerate the adoption of self-driving technology, including the ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Showcases
Autonomous Driving Progress at CES 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, is set to showcase its advanced autonomous driving solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, highlighting its commitment to innovation and safety. The company's intelligent driving division, Lotus Robotics, will present proprietary software and hardware, including the ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy, which enhance driver assistance, enable remote operations, and manage cloud data. These technologies are integral to Lotus Tech's vehicles, such as the hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, which feature cutting-edge hardware like dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips and a unique cognition module for risk management.

Recently, Lotus Tech received approval to test Level 3 automated driving on highways in Wuxi, China, a pilot zone for vehicle connectivity. This allows Eletre drivers to utilize its L3 capabilities on designated highways, supported by the vehicle's advanced Level 4-ready hardware, including a deployable LIDAR system and multiple sensors for enhanced perception. Lotus Robotics is also promoting self-driving technology through its ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

As part of its Vision80 business transformation strategy, Lotus Tech is enhancing its global R&D capabilities, with CEO Mr. Qingfeng Feng expressing optimism about the future of intelligent driving. The company is also nearing the completion of a merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, expected to finalize in early 2024, which will transition Lotus Tech to a public entity listed on Nasdaq under the ticker "LOT." Visitors can explore Lotus Robotics' innovations at booth 9217 in the Tech East AI pavilion from January 9-12.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In recognition of Lotus Technology Inc.’s leadership in intelligent driving, the Company recently received approval to test Level 3 autonomous driving in Wuxi, a city near Shanghai serving as China’s pilot zone for “internet of vehicles” connectivity. This license allows Eletre drivers to use the Eletre’s Level 3 functionality on designated highways in the city. The Eletre is equipped with state-of-the-art Level 4-ready hardware including the world’s first deployable LIDAR system, and leverages five different sensor types to supplement high-definition digital maps with live, 360° perception. “Lotus Robotics’ leading technologies bear testament to Lotus Technology Inc.’s world-class research and development innovation capabilities and signify a significant step forward in the Vision80 strategy. Lotus Technology Inc. looks forward to testing these innovative technologies on the roads in Wuxi as it strives to strengthen Lotus Technology Inc.’s intelligent driving capabilities and deliver even better, safer driving experiences to its customers around the world,” said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer of Lotus Technology Inc. Visit Lotus Robotics at the Las Vegas Convention Center’s North Hall, in the Tech East AI (Artificial Intelligence) pavilion at booth 9217 from January 9-12. As announced in January 2023, Lotus Technology Inc. has entered into a definitive agreement and plan of merger (the “Merger Agreement”) with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“LCAA”) (NASDAQ: LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology’s American Depositary Shares will begin trading on the Nasdaq on February 23, 2024, under the ticker symbol “LOT.” New York and Singapore - February 20, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“L CAA”) (NASDAQ: LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm, announced today that their previously proposed business combination is expected to be completed on February 22, 2024. The combined company will retain Lotus Technology Inc.'s name as “Lotus Technology Inc.” and its American Depositary Shares (ADS) will commence trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol “LOT” on February 23, 2024. The business combination was approved by L CAA shareholders at an extraordinary general meeting held on February 2, 2024. Integrating Lotus Technology’s sports car DNA with a new generation of premium lifestyle vehicles, Lotus Technology Inc. is committed to transforming the brand into an advanced, fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider before its 80th anniversary in 2028. Lotus Technology Inc. has already set this ambitious plan in motion by launching a leading product portfolio of high-performing battery electric vehicles (“BEVs”) ahead of other global luxury automakers. These BEVs include Eletre, a new breed of all-electric hyper-SUV powered by Lotus Technology’s proprietary 800-volt EPA, and Emeya, an all-electric hyper-GT among the most advanced vehicles of its kind in the world.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Tech and  L  Catterton Asia Acquisition
Corp Announce Closing of Business Combination 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, is poised to make its debut on the Nasdaq stock exchange on February 23, 2024, trading under the ticker symbol "LOT." This significant milestone follows the completion of its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA), which is set to finalize on February 22, 2024, after receiving shareholder approval on February 2, 2024. Lotus Tech aims to transform into a fully electric and sustainable luxury mobility provider by 2028, coinciding with its 80th anniversary, and plans to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027. The company has already introduced high-performing battery electric vehicles, such as the Eletre hyper-SUV and the Emeya hyper-GT, with plans for two additional models in the next two years. Since announcing the merger, Lotus Tech has secured over $880 million in financing commitments, marking one of the largest de-SPAC transactions since 2023. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed enthusiasm for the company's growth as a public entity, while L CAA Co-CEO Chinta Bhagat highlighted their shared vision for establishing a leading luxury electric vehicle brand. To commemorate its public listing, Lotus Tech will ring the Nasdaq opening bell on February 23, an event that will be live-streamed for viewers.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: With both BEVs already on the roads and two more fully electric models planned for launch over the next two years, Lotus Technology Inc. is on track to become the first traditional luxury automotive brand to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027. Since the transaction was announced, Lotus Technology Inc. has raised more than US$880 million in pre-closing and private investment in public equity (“PIPE”) financing commitments from global investors, existing shareholders, and strategic partners, representing one of the largest amounts of additional financings raised in connection with a de-SPAC transaction since 2023. “Lotus Technology Inc. is thrilled to announce its upcoming debut on the Nasdaq as it completes its business combination with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp,” said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer of Lotus Technology Inc. “This is a pivotal moment in our journey. Lotus Technology Inc. looks forward to accelerating its growth as a listed company, leading the electric transformation of the global luxury battery electric vehicle market together with L Catterton.” “L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp's partnership with Lotus Technology Inc. has gotten stronger over time and our respect for the mission Lotus Technology Inc. is on has only increased,” said Chinta Bhagat, Co-Chief Executive Officer of L CAA. “We are excited about the road ahead as we continue building the definitive luxury electric vehicle brand together, leveraging our global consumer insights and our strategic relationship with LVMH.

During the six months ended June 30, 2024, and 2023, these purchases amounted to US$521,836 and US$264,440, of which US$326,187 and US$118,714 were recognized as cost of goods sold for the six months ended June 30, 2024, and 2023, respectively. metadata.title_summary:
title: Note: 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Group Financial Activities and Related Party Transactions**

The Group is actively involved in the sale of sports cars, battery electric vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models, auto parts, and peripheral products, while also offering research and development (R&D) and consulting services to related parties. For the six-month period ending June 30, 2024, the Group reported service revenue of US$10,662 and product sales of US$2,330 to related parties, reflecting a substantial increase from US$5,021 and US$807, respectively, during the same period in 2023. As of June 30, 2024, accounts receivable from related parties amounted to US$19,674, a decrease from US$22,430 at the end of 2023. The Group's contract liabilities included receipts in advance totaling US$11,036, with a nominal amount of US$1 for the same periods.

In terms of financial transactions, the Group made payments on behalf of related parties totaling US$648 in 2024, down from US$1,405 in 2023. Cash receipts collected by related parties were reported at US$809 in 2024. Receivables in prepayments and other current assets related to these transactions stood at US$4,110 as of June 30, 2024. Notably, unsecured short-term loans to related parties were nil in 2024, a decrease from US$865 in 2023, which generated interest income of US$69 and US$57, respectively. A significant loan agreement was established on March 11, 2024, with a related party for US$354,000, of which US$222,865 was disbursed, resulting in an outstanding principal of US$219,644 and accrued interest of US$4,398 as of June 30, 2024.

The Group's prepayments for vehicle purchases increased to US$33,191 in 2024, compared to US$21,404 in 2023. Prepayments for equipment remained relatively stable at US$2,689 in 2024 versus US$2,706 in 2023. The Group's purchases from related parties surged to US$521,836 in 2024, a significant rise from US$264,440 in 2023, with US$326,187 recognized as cost of goods sold in 2024. As of June 30, 2024, inventory related to these purchases was recorded at US$166,318. Additionally, amounts due to related parties for various services were reported at US$15,599 as of June 30, 2024, compared to US$27,660 at the end of December 2023.

This summary encapsulates the Group's financial performance and related party transactions, highlighting significant increases in revenue, purchases, and prepayments, while also noting the changes in accounts receivable and amounts due to related parties.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology will need significant capital to, among other things, conduct research and development, expand its production capacity, and roll out its sales network, as well as delivery and servicing centers. As Lotus Technology ramps up its production capacity and operations, it may also require significant capital to maintain its property, plant, and equipment, and such costs may be greater than what the company currently anticipates. Lotus Technology expects that its level of capital expenditures will be significantly affected by consumer demand for its products and services. The fact that Lotus Technology has a limited operating history means it has limited historical data on the demand for its products and services. As a result, the company's future capital requirements may be uncertain, and actual capital requirements may be significantly different from what Lotus Technology currently anticipates. Lotus Technology may need to seek equity or debt financing to finance a portion of its capital expenditures. Such financing might not be available to the company in a timely manner or on terms that are acceptable, or at all. If Lotus Technology cannot obtain sufficient capital on acceptable terms, its business, financial condition, and prospects may be materially and adversely affected. Lotus Technology's ability to obtain the necessary financing to carry out its business plan is subject to a number of factors, including general market conditions and investor acceptance of its business plan. These factors may make the timing, amount, terms, and conditions of such financing unattractive or unavailable to Lotus Technology.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our business plans require a significant amount of capital. In addition, our future capital needs may require us to obtain additional equity or debt financing that may dilute our shareholders or introduce covenants that may restrict our operations or our ability to pay dividends. 
summary: The company is facing significant capital requirements for research and development, production expansion, and establishing a sales and service network. As production increases, the need for additional capital to maintain property and equipment may arise, influenced by unpredictable consumer demand due to the company's limited operational history. This uncertainty could lead to future capital needs that diverge from current estimates, necessitating potential equity or debt financing. However, securing such financing may be challenging due to market conditions and investor interest, which could result in unfavorable terms or timing. A failure to raise sufficient funds could force the company to cut spending, delay investments, or alter its structure, jeopardizing operations and potentially leading to a cessation of activities.

Additionally, the company has entered into put option agreements with Geely HK and Etika, allowing them to require the company to purchase their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd at a set price, contingent on the sale of over 5,000 vehicles in 2024. The exercise of these options, which can occur between April and June 2025, poses a risk of significant financial obligations that could negatively impact liquidity and overall financial health. Future capital raising efforts, whether through equity or debt, may lead to shareholder dilution or increased financial obligations, further complicating operational flexibility and dividend distributions.

However, uncertainty surrounds future consumer demand and capital requirements due to the company's limited operating history, which could lead to actual needs diverging from projections. To address these expenditures, the company may need to seek equity or debt financing, though there are risks associated with the availability and terms of such funding. Failure to secure sufficient capital could adversely affect the company's operations, financial condition, and growth prospects, potentially necessitating cost-cutting measures or restructuring.

Additionally, the company has entered into put option agreements with Geely HK and Etika, allowing them to require the company to buy back their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, contingent on the sale of over 5,000 vehicles in 2024. The exercise of these options could create significant financial obligations, impacting liquidity and overall financial health.

To meet future capital needs, the company may have to issue more equity or debt, which could dilute existing shareholders or increase financial burdens. Furthermore, the company faces risks related to its suppliers' adherence to ethical practices and compliance with laws, which could damage its brand reputation and lead to negative publicity. This situation raises concerns about potential operational disruptions and increased costs if non-compliance necessitates finding alternative suppliers. Overall, these factors could significantly impact the company's brand image, demand for its electric vehicles, and long-term business viability.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Share Incentive Plan 
summary: The "Share Incentive Plan" chapter outlines a structured incentive program aimed at aligning the interests of management with those of shareholders, thereby promoting retention and performance among key employees and executives. 

**Eligibility**: The plan is designed for key employees and executives, ensuring that those in pivotal roles are motivated to contribute to the company's success.

**Share Allocation**: A total of 1 million shares are reserved for the plan, with annual grants awarded based on performance metrics and individual contributions, fostering a performance-driven culture.

**Vesting Schedule**: Shares vest over a four-year period, with 25% of the shares vesting each year. This vesting is contingent upon the achievement of specific performance targets, encouraging sustained performance over time.

**Performance Metrics**: The plan employs several performance metrics to assess eligibility for share grants, including revenue growth, earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA), and total shareholder return (TSR). These metrics are designed to ensure that the interests of management are closely aligned with the overarching goals of the company.

**Regulatory Compliance**: The plan is structured to comply with relevant securities regulations, including the tax implications outlined under Section 409A of the Internal Revenue Code, ensuring that it meets legal standards and protects both the company and its employees.

**Amendments**: The board of directors retains the authority to amend or terminate the plan, although significant changes will require shareholder approval, ensuring a level of oversight and alignment with shareholder interests.

This comprehensive summary encapsulates the essential components of the Share Incentive Plan, highlighting its purpose, structure, regulatory compliance, and the mechanisms in place to align management's performance with shareholder value.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Related Party Transactions 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Related Party Transactions**

During the six months ended June 30, 2024, and the corresponding periods in 2023, as well as the fiscal years ending December 31 for 2023, 2022, and 2021, the company engaged in a variety of transactions with related parties, encompassing sales of sports cars, BEV lifestyle models, auto parts, and R&D consulting services. The following outlines the key financial metrics and transactions:

1. **Sales and Services Provided:**
   - Services rendered to related parties totaled **US$10.7 million** in 2024, **US$5.0 million** in 2023, **US$12.6 million** in 2022, **US$8.3 million** in 2021, and **US$3.3 million** in 2020.
   - Sales to related parties amounted to **US$2.3 million** in 2024, **US$0.8 million** in 2023, **US$3.4 million** in 2022, **US$23 thousand** in 2021, and **nil** in 2020.

2. **Accounts Receivable and Contract Liabilities:**
   - Accounts receivable from related parties were **US$19.7 million** as of June 30, 2024, **US$22.4 million** at December 31, 2023, and **US$8.5 million** in 2022.
   - Contract liabilities were recorded at **US$11.0 million** as of June 30, 2024, **US$1 thousand** in 2023, and **US$8 thousand** in 2022.

3. **Prepayments and Loans:**
   - Prepayments to related parties for vehicle purchases reached **US$33.2 million** in 2024, **US$21.4 million** in 2023, and **US$6.1 million** in 2022.
   - Unsecured short-term loans to related parties totaled **nil** in 2024, **US$865 thousand** in 2023, **US$1.2 million** in 2022, and **US$2.3 million** in 2021, with interest income recognized at **US$69 thousand** in 2024 and **US$57 thousand** in 2023.
   - A significant loan agreement was executed on **March 11, 2024**, involving **US$354.0 million** over five years at an interest rate of **6.95%**.

4. **Research and Development Expenses:**
   - R&D expenses from related parties were recorded at **US$38.3 million** in 2024, **US$40.3 million** in 2023, **US$134.7 million** in 2022, **US$167.0 million** in 2021, and **US$47.4 million** in 2020.

5. **Lease Agreements and Other Transactions:**
   - Short-term lease costs incurred were **US$474 thousand** in 2024, **US$63 thousand** in 2023, and **US$765 thousand** in 2022.
   - A distribution agreement with **Lotus Cars Limited** was established on **January 31, 2023**, for **GBP18.1 million** (approximately **US$22.3 million**).

6. **Equity and Licensing Transactions:**
   - The company acquired a non-exclusive license for the **Geely automotive chassis and technology platform** for **US$288.9 million**, which was expensed as R&D.
   - An equity transfer agreement for **US$15.5 million** was completed for acquiring **Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH** in June 2022.
   - A convertible note of **US$10.0 million** was purchased from **ECARX Holding Inc.**, converting into shares valued at **US$3.3 million** in 2023.

7. **Expected Credit Loss Provision:**
   - As of June 30, 2024, the expected credit loss provision for prepayments to related parties was **US$61 thousand**.

This comprehensive summary encapsulates the significant financial metrics and transactions with related parties, highlighting the substantial amounts and agreements made during the specified periods, while ensuring clarity and coherence in the presentation of the information.

Looking ahead, the company has ambitious plans for expansion, with new models scheduled for launch in 2025 and 2026. This strategic approach aims to solidify the company's position in the ultra-luxury automotive sector while addressing the evolving preferences of its clientele.

  - **2022**: US$244 thousand

  - **2022**: US$2.3 million

  - **2022**: US$24 thousand

  - **2022**: US$6.1 million

#### Purchases from Related Parties

metadata.title_summary:
title: NOTES TO THE UNAUDITED CONDENSED CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS (All amounts in thousands, except for share and per share data) 
summary: ### Comprehensive Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Unaudited Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements

**Overview:**
For the six months ended June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. reported substantial growth in total revenue, reaching **US$398,115** compared to **US$130,035** for the same period in 2023. This remarkable increase highlights the company's expanding market presence and operational success across various regions.

**Geographic Revenue Distribution:**
- **Chinese Mainland:** Revenue increased significantly to **US$138,210** in 2024 from **US$93,013** in 2023.
- **United Kingdom:** A notable surge in revenue to **US$69,728** in 2024, up from **US$7,396** in 2023.
- **Thailand:** Revenue rose to **US$24,594** in 2024, a substantial increase from just **US$248** in 2023.
- **Malaysia:** Revenue grew to **US$23,652** in 2024, compared to **US$1,040** in 2023.
- **Belgium:** Generated **US$21,155** in revenue in 2024, with no prior year data available.
- **Italy:** Revenue reached **US$17,522** in 2024, with no prior year data.
- **Japan:** Slight revenue increase to **US$16,943** in 2024 from **US$16,217** in 2023.
- **Germany:** Revenue of **US$14,951** in 2024, with no prior year data.
- **Netherlands:** Revenue totaled **US$11,145** in 2024, with no prior year data.
- **France:** Generated **US$10,553** in revenue in 2024, with no prior year data.
- **Other Regions:** Revenue totaled **US$49,662** in 2024, up from **US$12,121** in 2023.

**Contract Liabilities:**
As of June 30, 2024, total contract liabilities amounted to **US$68,114**, an increase from **US$50,430** as of December 31, 2023. The breakdown of contract liabilities is as follows:
- **Current Liabilities (Third Parties):** Increased to **US$50,054** in 2024 from **US$44,184** in 2023.
- **Current Liabilities (Related Parties):** Rose to **US$11,036** in 2024 from **US$1** in 2023.
- **Non-Current Liabilities (Third Parties):** Increased to **US$7,024** in 2024 from **US$6,245** in 2023.

During the six months ended June 30, 2024, **US$16,915** of the contract liabilities recognized as of December 31, 2023, was recognized as revenue. The Group anticipates recognizing **US$255,159** of the transaction price allocated to unsatisfied performance obligations within one year, with the remaining **US$7,045** expected to be recognized over the next 1 to 10 years.

**Additional Notes:**
- All figures are presented in thousands of US dollars (US$).
- The calculation of loss per share is related to put options.
- Contract liabilities arise from up-front payments for vehicle or service purchases prior to the transfer of control.

This summary encapsulates the key financial performance metrics and contract liabilities of Lotus Technology Inc. for the first half of 2024, reflecting the company's growth trajectory and operational dynamics in various geographic markets.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

title: SIGNATURES 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has filed a report in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, officially signed by an authorized representative. On January 14, 2025, the company announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for 2024, achieving a significant milestone with 12,065 vehicles delivered, reflecting over 70% year-on-year growth. This performance established Lotus as the fastest-growing luxury brand, with approximately 25% of deliveries originating from China. Looking ahead, Lotus aims to increase global deliveries by 20% in 2025, with China's contribution expected to rise to 25-28%. The company has also regained proprietary rights to its trademarks, including the iconic LOTUS roundel and the Chinese character for Lotus ("莲花"), reinforcing its global branding consistency. The reported delivery figures encompass invoiced and commissioned deliveries in the US, based on submissions from various business units, and may differ slightly from the company's financial records. Additionally, the summary highlights the competitive landscape, referencing prestigious luxury automotive brands such as Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren, known for their exceptional craftsmanship and appeal to elite car enthusiasts.

How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 1. How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year?
summary: In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology demonstrated exceptional growth, reporting total revenue of $398 million, which reflects a remarkable year-on-year increase of 206%. The company's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained robust, exceeding $100,000, and significantly outpacing growth in both the automotive industry and the luxury car segment. This surge in revenue was accompanied by an improvement in gross margin, which rose to 13%, up from 5% during the same period in 2023.

Lotus achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, marking a staggering 239% increase year-on-year, thereby establishing itself as the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car manufacturers. Deliveries were well-distributed across major markets, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of World each contributing between 20-30% to the total.

The company has also embarked on strategic collaborations with leading automotive brands, which are expected to enhance technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and technology development services. The growth in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware is anticipated to create additional opportunities for paid software subscription revenue.

Furthermore, the introduction of the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service has allowed Lotus to offer personalized options and designer customizations, catering to evolving customer demands. This initiative is expected to strengthen the brand's luxury positioning and positively influence long-term profit margins.

Overall, Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of 2024 reflects strong financial growth, strategic market positioning, and a commitment to enhancing customer experience, setting a solid foundation for continued success in the luxury automotive sector.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Announces Delivery Updates
of First Ten Months of 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has reported its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024, achieving a total of 8,631 vehicles delivered. The primary markets driving these deliveries were Europe and China, which accounted for approximately 35% and 25% of total deliveries, respectively. In October, the company launched an open beta test for its urban Navigate on Autopilot (NOA) feature in key cities and introduced several over-the-air (OTA) intelligent driving functions in Europe. 

Additionally, Lotus Robotics, the company's division focused on intelligent driving solutions, is providing services not only for Lotus vehicles but also for other major automotive manufacturers, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto. The company has also been recognized for its commitment to sustainability, receiving the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category, making it the only automotive industry recipient of this accolade.

It is important to note that the delivery figures are based on invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the U.S. market, and may differ slightly from the company's financial accounting records. Overall, Lotus Technology continues to make significant strides in vehicle delivery, technological advancements, and sustainability initiatives.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – Oct 17, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 7,543 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of 134%. Global allocation in terms of deliveries remained well-distributed, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World regions each contributing about 20-35% of the total deliveries. In the third quarter of 2024, the Emeya (GT, Sedan) was delivered to Europe, whilst the Eletre (SUV) reached markets including the GCC, Japan, and Korea. In September, Lotus Technology Inc. revealed the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market with a starting price of $229,900. The deliveries of the Eletre Carbon are expected to commence in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter.

summary: Lotus Technology Inc. announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024 on October 17, 2024, reporting a total of 7,543 vehicles delivered, which represents a remarkable 134% increase compared to the same period last year. The deliveries were well-distributed across global markets, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World each contributing approximately 20-35% to the overall delivery figures.

In the third quarter of 2024, the company successfully delivered the Emeya (GT, Sedan) in Europe, while the Eletre (SUV) expanded its reach to markets including the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC), Japan, and Korea. Notably, the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant specifically designed for the North American market, was revealed in September 2024, with a starting price set at $229,900. Deliveries for this model are anticipated to commence in the first half of 2025. Additionally, bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre and Emeya began their deliveries in Q3 2024.

Lotus also showcased the Theory 1 concept car in September, highlighting its commitment to advanced technology and eco-friendly features. It is important to note that the delivery figures provided are based on reports from business units and may differ from those recorded in financial accounting systems. This comprehensive overview underscores Lotus Technology's significant growth trajectory and its strategic expansion into various international markets.

summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated significant growth and a strong commitment to expanding its global presence, as evidenced by its financial performance and strategic initiatives. The company reported an impressive 811% increase in revenue year-over-year for the first quarter of 2024, reaching $173 million, driven by a substantial rise in vehicle deliveries. Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is navigating a complex landscape of rapid growth and financial challenges, while maintaining its legacy in automotive excellence and motorsports. The company's strategic focus on expanding its market presence and improving operational efficiency positions it for potential future success, despite current financial hurdles.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Over 7,600 vehicles were delivered in the first nine months of 2024, a year-on-year increase of 136%. Total revenue reached $653 million, more than doubling year-on-year. Revenue from the intelligent driving business generated from customers other than Lotus surged to $11 million, with a year-on-year growth of 450%. Lotus Technology launched Hyper Hybrid EV technology that provides high performance with a combined range of over 1,100 kilometers. New York – Nov 21, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and first nine months ended September 30, 2024.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Third
Quarter and First Nine Months 2024 Financial Results 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has released its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and the first nine months of 2024, concluding on September 30, 2024. During this period, the company achieved a remarkable milestone by delivering over 7,600 vehicles, which represents a substantial year-on-year increase of 136%. Total revenue for the company reached $653 million, more than doubling compared to the same period last year. 

A significant highlight of the financial results was the performance of the intelligent driving business, which generated $11 million in revenue from customers other than Lotus, showcasing an impressive year-on-year growth of 450%. 

In addition to these financial achievements, Lotus Technology announced the launch of its Hyper Hybrid EV technology on November 21, 2024. This innovative technology promises high performance with a combined driving range exceeding 1,100 kilometers, further solidifying the company's commitment to advancing electric vehicle technology.

Overall, Lotus Technology's third-quarter and nine-month results reflect strong growth and innovation within the company, positioning it favorably in the competitive automotive market.
